disallowed_assumptions: password stored in plain text
auto-approve all logins
no session expiry
review_notes: Must cover the core happy path. Session token creation is explicitly required.

gold_test_cases.title: Login succeeds with valid credentials
preconditions: User account exists
User is not already logged in
gold_test_cases.expected_result: User is authenticated and redirected to their personal dashboard

gold_test_cases.title: Session token is created on successful login
preconditions: User account exists
gold_test_cases.expected_result: A secure session token is present in storage after successful login

disallowed_assumptions: account locked permanently after first failure
password hint shown
email existence confirmed in error
review_notes: Security-sensitive. Must not confirm email existence. Logging is explicitly required.

gold_test_cases.title: Login failure shows generic error message
preconditions: User account exists with known email
steps: Enter valid email with incorrect password
gold_test_cases.expected_result: A generic error message is displayed that does not indicate whether the email exists

gold_test_cases.title: Failed login attempt is logged
preconditions: User account exists
steps: Submit login form with incorrect password
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The failed login attempt is recorded in the system log with timestamp

gold_test_cases.title: Password field is cleared after failed login
preconditions: User is on the login page
steps: Enter any email and wrong password
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The password field is empty after the failed attempt

disallowed_assumptions: regular users can delete projects
no confirmation required
deletion is reversible
review_notes: Permission check, confirmation UX, cascading delete, and notification are all required by spec.

gold_test_cases.title: Admin can delete a project after confirmation
preconditions: Admin user is logged in
At least one project exists
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The project and all its tasks are permanently removed from the system

gold_test_cases.title: Confirmation dialog is shown before project deletion
preconditions: Admin user is logged in
A project exists
gold_test_cases.expected_result: A confirmation dialog is displayed before any deletion occurs

gold_test_cases.title: Project members are notified when a project is deleted
preconditions: Admin user is logged in
A project with members exists
gold_test_cases.expected_result: All project members receive a notification that the project has been deleted

gold_test_cases.title: Non-admin user cannot delete a project
preconditions: Member role user is logged in
A project exists
gold_test_cases.expected_result: No delete option is visible or accessible to the member role user

disallowed_assumptions: task requires assignee to save
auto-assigned to creator
unassigned tasks are hidden
review_notes: Edge case: creation without required-ish field. Explicit spec says assignee is optional and can be added later.

gold_test_cases.title: Task created successfully with no assignee
preconditions: User is logged in
A project exists
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Task is created and saved with assignee shown as Unassigned

gold_test_cases.title: Unassigned task appears in unassigned view
preconditions: An unassigned task exists
steps: Navigate to team unassigned task view
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The unassigned task is visible in the unassigned task view

gold_test_cases.title: Assignee can be added to a task after creation
preconditions: An unassigned task exists
steps: Set assignee to a team member
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The task is updated with the selected assignee

disallowed_assumptions: existing account overwritten
silent failure with no message
user is auto-logged-in to existing account
review_notes: Simple uniqueness validation. Must confirm existing account is not touched.

gold_test_cases.title: Registration with duplicate email shows error
preconditions: A user account with email test@example.com exists
gold_test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed indicating the email address is already in use

gold_test_cases.title: Duplicate email registration does not modify existing account
preconditions: User account for test@example.com exists with known password
steps: Attempt registration with test@example.com and a different password
Try logging in with the original password
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The existing account password is unchanged; original user can still log in

disallowed_assumptions: link never expires
link can be used multiple times
reset without email verification
review_notes: Expiry (60 min), single-use, and old-password invalidation are all explicitly stated and must be covered.

gold_test_cases.title: Password reset link is sent to registered email
preconditions: User account exists
gold_test_cases.expected_result: A password reset email is sent to the provided address

gold_test_cases.title: Password reset link expires after 60 minutes
preconditions: A password reset link has been issued
steps: Wait until link is older than 60 minutes
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The link is rejected with an expiry message

gold_test_cases.title: Password reset link can only be used once
preconditions: A valid unexpired password reset link exists
steps: Use the link to reset the password successfully
Attempt to use the same link again
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The second use of the link is rejected

gold_test_cases.title: Old password is invalidated after reset
preconditions: User has completed a password reset
steps: Attempt to log in using the old password
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Login with the old password fails

disallowed_assumptions: member users have admin access
silent redirect without any message
audit log not required
review_notes: Three explicit outcomes: denied response, redirect, audit log. All three must be covered.

gold_test_cases.title: Member user is denied access to admin dashboard
preconditions: Member role user is logged in
steps: Navigate directly to /admin or admin dashboard URL
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Access is denied with an appropriate error response

gold_test_cases.title: Member user is redirected after denied admin access
preconditions: Member role user is logged in
steps: Attempt to access admin dashboard URL
gold_test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to their own dashboard after being denied access

gold_test_cases.title: Unauthorized admin access attempt is recorded in audit log
preconditions: Member role user is logged in
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The access attempt is recorded in the audit log with user identity and timestamp

disallowed_assumptions: special chars cause 500 error
SQL query constructed by string concatenation
unescaped output returned
review_notes: Security-critical. Must explicitly test injection-style inputs. Requires both input sanitization and safe output rendering.

gold_test_cases.title: Search with SQL metacharacters returns safe results
preconditions: User is logged in
Tasks exist in the system
steps: Enter a search query containing SQL metacharacters such as quote OR 1=1 --
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Search returns results without a server error and without exposing database errors

gold_test_cases.title: Search query with angle brackets is displayed safely
preconditions: User is logged in
steps: Enter a search query containing script tags
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The search term is displayed back to the user with angle brackets encoded and no script executes

gold_test_cases.title: Search with ampersands and quotes returns results gracefully
preconditions: User is logged in
steps: Enter a search term containing ampersands and double quotes
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Search completes without error and returns relevant results or an empty result set

disallowed_assumptions: all users receive all notifications with no opt-out
notifications sent to third parties without consent
notification content contains sensitive data
review_notes: Requirement is severely under-specified. Tester must flag ambiguity and avoid inventing system details. Good output will note coverage gaps in notes field.

gold_test_cases.title: User receives notification for relevant event
preconditions: User account exists
User has not disabled notifications
steps: Check user notification inbox or email
gold_test_cases.expected_result: User receives a notification via the configured channel

gold_test_cases.title: User can opt out of notifications
preconditions: User is logged in
gold_test_cases.expected_result: User does not receive a notification after opting out

disallowed_assumptions: premium features accessible without payment
charges applied without user confirmation
upgrade is instantaneous with no review step
review_notes: Requirement lacks specifics on payment flow, feature list, and pricing. Good output will test core billing integrity and flag gaps.

gold_test_cases.title: User must provide payment method to upgrade
preconditions: User is logged in with a free account
steps: Attempt to complete upgrade without entering payment details
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The system requires payment method entry before proceeding

gold_test_cases.title: Upgrade confirmation is shown before charging
preconditions: User is on upgrade page with payment details entered
steps: Check for confirmation step before final charge
gold_test_cases.expected_result: A confirmation page or dialog is shown summarizing the charge before it is applied

gold_test_cases.title: Premium features are accessible after successful upgrade
preconditions: User has completed a successful premium upgrade
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The premium feature is accessible to the upgraded user

disallowed_assumptions: access granted without TOTP when MFA is enabled
TOTP bypass via URL manipulation
code reusable after first use
review_notes: MFA is a multi-step flow. Must cover both the happy path (correct TOTP after correct password) and negative paths (wrong code, expired code).

gold_test_cases.title: User with MFA enabled is prompted for TOTP after password
preconditions: User account exists with MFA enabled
User is not logged in
steps: Enter valid email and correct password
gold_test_cases.expected_result: System prompts for a TOTP code and does not grant access yet

gold_test_cases.title: Valid TOTP code grants access
preconditions: User has entered valid password and is on TOTP prompt
steps: Enter a currently valid TOTP code from authenticator app
gold_test_cases.expected_result: User is authenticated and redirected to their dashboard

gold_test_cases.title: Invalid TOTP code is rejected
preconditions: User has entered valid password and is on TOTP prompt
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Error is displayed indicating the code is invalid; access is not granted

required_coverage_points: new session ID issued on login
old session token rejected after login
disallowed_assumptions: session ID unchanged after login
old session still valid after authentication
session fixation is acceptable
review_notes: Session fixation prevention is a security control. The key observable is that the session ID before login differs from the session ID after login, AND the old session no longer works.

gold_test_cases.title: Session ID changes upon successful authentication
preconditions: An unauthenticated session exists (e.g. browsing the login page)
steps: Record the session ID from the pre-login cookie
Submit valid credentials and complete login
Inspect the session cookie after login
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The session ID after login is different from the session ID that existed before login

gold_test_cases.title: Pre-authentication session token is invalidated after login
preconditions: User holds a session token from before authentication
steps: Attempt to use the old pre-login session token in a subsequent request
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The old session token is rejected and the request is treated as unauthenticated

disallowed_assumptions: OAuth bypasses email verification entirely
accounts auto-merged without confirmation on email conflict
OAuth tokens stored indefinitely
review_notes: Standard OAuth flow. Must cover success path, account linking, and OAuth provider failure. Flag if model invents specific provider behavior not stated.

gold_test_cases.title: User is redirected to OAuth provider on sign-in click
preconditions: User is on the login page
steps: Click the sign in with OAuth provider button
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Browser is redirected to the OAuth provider authorization page

gold_test_cases.title: New account is created after successful OAuth authorization
preconditions: No account exists for the OAuth provider identity
gold_test_cases.expected_result: A new user account is created and the user is redirected to their dashboard

gold_test_cases.title: OAuth provider failure shows an error to the user
preconditions: User initiates OAuth sign-in
steps: Simulate OAuth provider returning an error or the user denying access
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Application displays an error message; user is not logged in

disallowed_assumptions: viewer can edit their own comments
viewer write attempts are silently ignored
viewer has same rights as editor
review_notes: Must test both positive (read allowed) and negative (write blocked) paths. Four distinct write actions blocked.

gold_test_cases.title: Viewer can view tasks in a project
preconditions: User has viewer role in a project
Tasks exist in the project
steps: Navigate to the project task list
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Tasks are displayed and task details are accessible

gold_test_cases.title: Viewer cannot create a task
preconditions: User has viewer role in a project
steps: Attempt to create a new task via API or UI
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Request is rejected with an access-denied error; no task is created

gold_test_cases.title: Viewer cannot edit a task
preconditions: User has viewer role
A task exists
steps: Attempt to edit the task title or status
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Edit is rejected with an access-denied error

gold_test_cases.title: Viewer cannot delete a task
preconditions: User has viewer role
A task exists
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Delete is rejected with an access-denied error

disallowed_assumptions: editors can delete projects
editors can modify project-level settings
editor role same as admin
review_notes: Straightforward permission test. Cover both allowed actions and the one explicit prohibition.

gold_test_cases.title: Editor can create a new task
preconditions: User has editor role in a project
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Task is created successfully

gold_test_cases.title: Editor can edit an existing task
preconditions: User has editor role
A task exists
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Task is updated successfully

gold_test_cases.title: Editor cannot delete the project
preconditions: User has editor role in a project
steps: Attempt to access delete project option or call delete project API
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Delete project action is denied with an access-denied error

required_coverage_points: org admin can manage all projects
org admin can manage org membership
project admin limited to own project
project admin cannot access other projects
disallowed_assumptions: project admin has org-wide permissions
org admin cannot delete projects
role boundaries enforced only at UI level
review_notes: Multi-condition role boundary test. Must cover both org admin capabilities AND project admin limitations. Requires at least two separate projects to test scope isolation.

gold_test_cases.title: Org admin can manage all projects in the organization
preconditions: Org admin is logged in
Multiple projects exist
steps: Perform an admin action such as changing project name
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Org admin can access and modify settings for all projects in the organization

gold_test_cases.title: Org admin can manage organization membership
preconditions: Org admin is logged in
At least one user exists
steps: Add or remove a user from the organization
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Org admin successfully manages user membership at the organization level

gold_test_cases.title: Project admin cannot access a project they do not administer
preconditions: User is a project admin for Project A but not Project B
steps: Attempt to access Project B settings or admin functions
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Access to Project B admin functions is denied

gold_test_cases.title: Project admin cannot modify org-level membership
preconditions: User is a project-level admin only
steps: Attempt to navigate to organization membership settings
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Organization membership settings are not accessible to the project admin

required_coverage_points: active session denied on next request
disallowed_assumptions: removed user retains access until next login
grace period allowed after removal
removal only takes effect on session expiry
review_notes: State-dependent requirement. Must test that an already-active session loses access immediately, not just that new logins are prevented. The no-grace-period constraint is explicit.

gold_test_cases.title: Removed user cannot access project tasks
preconditions: User was a project member
Admin removes the user from the project
steps: Attempt to access the project or its tasks after removal
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Access is denied with an appropriate error response

gold_test_cases.title: Active session is denied on next project request after removal
preconditions: User has an active authenticated session
Admin removes the user from the project mid-session
steps: User makes a request to a project resource after removal
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Request is rejected with access denied and user is redirected; access is revoked without waiting for session expiry

gold_test_cases.title: Access revocation takes effect immediately with no grace period
preconditions: User is an active project member
steps: User immediately attempts to access project content
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Access is denied immediately; no grace period is observed

disallowed_assumptions: partial bulk updates accepted without error
bulk delete without any confirmation
operations applied one at a time with individual confirmations
review_notes: Multi-operation requirement. Must cover all three bulk actions plus the confirmation UX for delete. Atomic update is an explicit constraint.

gold_test_cases.title: User can select multiple tasks with checkboxes
preconditions: User is logged in
Multiple tasks exist in a project
steps: Click checkboxes on two or more tasks
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Multiple tasks are selected and a bulk action toolbar appears

gold_test_cases.title: Bulk reassign updates all selected tasks
preconditions: Multiple tasks are selected
gold_test_cases.expected_result: All selected tasks are reassigned to the new user

gold_test_cases.title: Bulk delete shows confirmation dialog with count
preconditions: Multiple tasks are selected
gold_test_cases.expected_result: A confirmation dialog appears showing the number of tasks to be deleted

gold_test_cases.title: Bulk status change updates all selected tasks
preconditions: Multiple tasks are selected
gold_test_cases.expected_result: All selected tasks are updated to the new status

required_coverage_points: overdue indicator shown when past due and not done
disallowed_assumptions: due date is required for task creation
overdue indicator shown on completed tasks
overdue calculated from creation date not due date
review_notes: Two-condition logic: overdue indicator requires BOTH past-due date AND not-done status. Completed tasks explicitly exempt.

gold_test_cases.title: Task can be created without a due date
preconditions: User is logged in
A project exists
steps: Create a new task without entering a due date
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Task is created successfully with no due date shown

gold_test_cases.title: Overdue indicator appears when due date is past and task is not done
preconditions: A task exists with a due date set to yesterday
Task status is not done
steps: View the task in the task list
gold_test_cases.expected_result: An overdue indicator is displayed on the task

gold_test_cases.title: Completed task does not show overdue indicator even if due date is past
preconditions: A task exists with a past due date
Task status is done
gold_test_cases.expected_result: No overdue indicator is displayed on the completed task

required_coverage_points: each subtask has title and status
all subtasks done does not auto-complete parent
disallowed_assumptions: completing all subtasks automatically completes the parent task
subtasks require due dates
subtasks have all fields of a regular task
review_notes: The explicit anti-pattern is auto-completing the parent. Must be tested. Progress ratio display is an explicit output requirement.

gold_test_cases.title: User can add a subtask to an existing task
preconditions: User is logged in
A task exists
steps: Add a new subtask with a title
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The subtask appears under the parent task

gold_test_cases.title: Parent task shows subtask progress ratio
preconditions: A task exists with 3 subtasks, 2 of which are completed
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Parent task displays a progress indicator showing 2/3 subtasks completed

gold_test_cases.title: Completing all subtasks does not auto-complete the parent task
preconditions: A task exists with multiple subtasks
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Parent task status remains unchanged; it is not automatically set to done

disallowed_assumptions: all results returned in one response regardless of count
page size configurable by user
pagination not needed for small result sets
review_notes: Standard pagination requirement. Cover page size, metadata in response, and navigation.

gold_test_cases.title: Search returns 25 results per page
preconditions: More than 25 tasks exist matching a search query
steps: Perform a search that returns more than 25 results
Inspect the first page of results
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Exactly 25 results are returned on the first page

gold_test_cases.title: Search response includes total result count and page number
preconditions: A search returns multiple pages of results
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Response includes the total number of matching results and the current page number

gold_test_cases.title: User can navigate to a specific page of search results
preconditions: Search returns more than 50 results
steps: Navigate to page 2 of search results
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Page 2 results are displayed, containing the next 25 results

required_coverage_points: tasks exist without filters returns results
disallowed_assumptions: OR logic between different filter types
filters are mutually exclusive
combined filters multiply results rather than restrict them
review_notes: Multi-condition logic test. AND semantics must be explicitly tested with two or more active filters. The no-filter baseline test prevents false confidence in filter correctness.

gold_test_cases.title: Filter by status returns only matching tasks
preconditions: Tasks exist with different statuses
steps: Apply status filter set to in_progress
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Only tasks with status in_progress are returned

gold_test_cases.title: Combined status and assignee filters apply AND logic
preconditions: Tasks exist with various status and assignee combinations
steps: Apply status filter for done and assignee filter for a specific user
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Only tasks that are both done AND assigned to that user are returned

gold_test_cases.title: Due date range filter returns only tasks within range
preconditions: Tasks exist with different due dates
steps: Apply a due date range filter covering a specific week
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Only tasks with due dates within the specified range are returned

gold_test_cases.title: No active filters returns all existing tasks
preconditions: Tasks exist in the project
steps: Perform search or task list view with no filters applied
gold_test_cases.expected_result: All existing tasks are returned in the results

required_coverage_points: events batched into single daily email
includes all events since last digest

no email if no events: skip email if nothing to report

disallowed_assumptions: digest sent even when no events occurred
immediate notifications still sent in digest mode
digest time cannot be configured
review_notes: The no-send-if-empty behavior is an explicit requirement. Configurable delivery time must be covered.

gold_test_cases.title: User can enable digest mode for notifications
preconditions: User is logged in with notification settings accessible
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Digest mode is enabled and the delivery time preference is saved

gold_test_cases.title: Events are batched into a single daily digest email
preconditions: User has digest mode enabled
Multiple events occurred since last digest
steps: Wait until configured digest delivery time
gold_test_cases.expected_result: A single digest email is received containing all events since the previous digest

gold_test_cases.title: No digest email is sent when no events occurred
preconditions: User has digest mode enabled
No events occurred since last digest
steps: Wait until configured digest delivery time
gold_test_cases.expected_result: No digest email is sent for that period

required_coverage_points: disabling one channel does not affect others
disallowed_assumptions: disabling email disables all notification channels
preference changes have delayed effect
channels cannot be individually controlled
review_notes: Independence between channels is the key invariant to test. Must verify that disabling channel A does not affect channel B.

gold_test_cases.title: User can independently disable email notifications
preconditions: All notification channels are enabled
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Email notifications are disabled; in-app and push notifications remain enabled

gold_test_cases.title: Disabling push notifications does not affect in-app notifications
preconditions: User has push and in-app notifications enabled
gold_test_cases.expected_result: In-app notification is received; push notification is not sent

gold_test_cases.title: Channel preference change takes effect immediately
preconditions: User enables a previously disabled channel
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Notification is delivered via the newly enabled channel without delay

required_coverage_points: user can delete excess to restore access
disallowed_assumptions: content deleted automatically on downgrade
premium features remain accessible after downgrade
user given unlimited grace period to reduce usage
review_notes: Complex state-dependent requirement with multiple explicit outcomes. Auto-deletion without user action is explicitly prohibited. Both the read-only enforcement AND the path to restore access must be tested.

gold_test_cases.title: Premium features become inaccessible immediately after downgrade
preconditions: User is on premium plan
User confirms downgrade to free tier
steps: Attempt to access a premium-only feature
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The premium feature is inaccessible immediately after downgrade confirmation

gold_test_cases.title: Projects exceeding free tier limit become read-only after downgrade
preconditions: User has 5 active projects and downgrades to a plan with a 3-project limit
steps: Attempt to edit a task in one of the excess projects
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The excess projects are read-only; editing is not allowed

gold_test_cases.title: User is shown which items are affected by the downgrade
preconditions: User downgrade would exceed free tier limits
gold_test_cases.expected_result: User sees a clear indication of which projects or content items are affected

gold_test_cases.title: Deleting excess projects restores write access
preconditions: User has downgraded and excess projects are read-only
steps: Delete the excess projects to bring usage within free tier limits
Attempt to edit a task in a remaining project
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Write access is restored to the remaining projects after excess projects are removed

required_coverage_points: PDF invoice generated per billing period
invoice contains user name and billing address
invoice contains plan name and period
invoice contains amount and invoice number
available in billing history and downloadable
disallowed_assumptions: invoice not generated for first billing period
invoice lacks required fields
invoice only available as HTML
review_notes: Field completeness is the primary risk. Cover all six required fields and verify download accessibility.

gold_test_cases.title: PDF invoice is generated after a billing period completes
preconditions: User is on a paid plan
A billing period has completed
gold_test_cases.expected_result: An invoice for the completed billing period is present and downloadable as PDF

gold_test_cases.title: Invoice contains all required fields
preconditions: A completed invoice exists
steps: Download and open the invoice PDF
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Invoice contains: user name, billing address, plan name, billing period dates, amount charged, and a unique invoice number

gold_test_cases.title: Invoice is accessible in billing history
preconditions: At least one completed billing period exists
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Invoice is listed in billing history and the download link is functional

disallowed_assumptions: rate limit shared across all users globally
no 429 response when limit exceeded
rate limit applies only to POST requests
review_notes: Must cover both the success path (requests under limit) and the limit-exceeded path including the Retry-After header. IP-based limiting for unauthenticated users is an explicit secondary requirement.

gold_test_cases.title: API requests under rate limit succeed normally
preconditions: Authenticated user with no prior requests this minute
steps: Send 10 API requests within one minute
gold_test_cases.expected_result: All requests return successful responses without rate limit errors

gold_test_cases.title: Exceeding rate limit returns HTTP 429 with Retry-After header
preconditions: Authenticated user
steps: Send more than 100 API requests within one minute
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Requests beyond the limit return HTTP 429 Too Many Requests with a Retry-After header

gold_test_cases.title: Unauthenticated requests are rate limited by IP address
preconditions: Multiple unauthenticated requests from the same IP address
steps: Send more than the allowed unauthenticated request limit from one IP
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Requests from that IP exceeding the limit receive HTTP 429 responses

required_coverage_points: deprecated version returns valid response plus deprecation header
deprecated version returns sunset date header
disallowed_assumptions: versioning via query parameter only
deprecated version returns error instead of valid response
unsupported version returns 410 Gone
review_notes: Three distinct scenarios: active version, deprecated version, and unsupported version. Deprecated still works but warns; unsupported does not work.

gold_test_cases.title: Active API version responds to requests normally
preconditions: API v2 is the current active version
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Request succeeds with a normal response and no deprecation headers

gold_test_cases.title: Deprecated API version returns valid response with Deprecation header
preconditions: API v1 is deprecated but still active
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Request returns a valid response body plus a Deprecation response header

gold_test_cases.title: Deprecated API version response includes X-API-Sunset-Date header
preconditions: API v1 is deprecated
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Response includes an X-API-Sunset-Date header indicating when the version will be removed

gold_test_cases.title: Unsupported API version returns HTTP 404
preconditions: No API v99 exists
gold_test_cases.expected_result: HTTP 404 Not Found is returned

disallowed_assumptions: different error structures for different endpoints
HTML error responses returned
error_code is a numeric status code not a string
review_notes: Consistency across all endpoints is the key claim. Must test both a 4xx scenario and a 5xx scenario to verify status code alignment.

gold_test_cases.title: Client error response contains all three required fields
preconditions: API is running
steps: Send a request that triggers a client error such as 400 Bad Request
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Response body is JSON containing error_code, message, and request_id fields

gold_test_cases.title: Server error response contains all three required fields
preconditions: A condition that triggers a server error exists
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Response body contains error_code, message, and request_id with a 5xx HTTP status

gold_test_cases.title: Client error uses 4xx HTTP status code
preconditions: API is running
steps: Send a request with a missing required field
gold_test_cases.expected_result: HTTP status code is in the 400-499 range

cursor used to retrieve pages: pass cursor to get next page

disallowed_assumptions: offset-based pagination instead of cursor
page number parameter used
total_count required for cursor pagination to work
review_notes: Cursor semantics are specific: null next_cursor = last page, not empty results. Both next and prev cursors must be present.

gold_test_cases.title: List endpoint response includes next_cursor and prev_cursor
preconditions: More than one page of results exists
steps: Request the first page of tasks from a list endpoint
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Response includes both next_cursor and prev_cursor fields in the envelope

gold_test_cases.title: Passing next_cursor returns the following page of results
preconditions: First page has been retrieved and next_cursor is present
steps: Send a new request using the next_cursor value from the previous response
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The next page of results is returned

gold_test_cases.title: next_cursor is null or absent on the last page
preconditions: The last page of results is being retrieved
steps: Request the final page of results
gold_test_cases.expected_result: next_cursor is null or absent in the response, indicating no further pages

required_coverage_points: single quote in filter handled safely
disallowed_assumptions: filter values passed directly to SQL query without parameterization
database error messages acceptable in response
metacharacters cause expected 400 errors rather than being handled
review_notes: Security-critical adversarial test. The distinction between a safe 200/empty response and a dangerous 500/DB-error response is the key signal. Must test multiple injection vectors.

gold_test_cases.title: Filter with single quote returns safe response
preconditions: API task filter endpoint is accessible
Tasks exist
steps: Send GET /tasks?filter=task' with a single quote in the filter value
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Response is a 200 with matching tasks or empty array; no 500 error or database error message

gold_test_cases.title: SQL comment sequence in filter does not cause error or injection
preconditions: API task filter endpoint is accessible
steps: Send a filter value containing -- or /**/ SQL comment sequences
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Response is safe (200 with results or empty); no SQL is injected or executed

gold_test_cases.title: Semicolon in filter value is handled safely
preconditions: API task filter endpoint is accessible
steps: Send a filter value containing a semicolon such as done; DROP TABLE tasks--
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Response is 200 with normal results; database is unaffected

required_coverage_points: bearer token required for protected endpoints
disallowed_assumptions: protected endpoints accessible without a token
expired tokens treated as valid
401 returned for all unauthenticated requests including /health
review_notes: Two explicit exemptions (/health and /auth/token) must be tested as positive cases. All other endpoints must require auth.

gold_test_cases.title: Request without Bearer token to protected endpoint returns 401
preconditions: API is running
steps: Send GET /tasks without an Authorization header
gold_test_cases.expected_result: HTTP 401 Unauthorized is returned

gold_test_cases.title: Request with expired token returns 401
preconditions: An expired JWT or session token is available
gold_test_cases.expected_result: HTTP 401 Unauthorized is returned with an error indicating token expiry

gold_test_cases.title: /health endpoint is accessible without authentication
preconditions: API is running
steps: Send GET /health without an Authorization header
gold_test_cases.expected_result: HTTP 200 is returned; endpoint is accessible without credentials

gold_test_cases.title: /auth/token endpoint is accessible without authentication
preconditions: API is running
steps: Send POST /auth/token with valid credentials but no pre-existing token
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Request is processed normally; endpoint does not require an existing Bearer token

required_coverage_points: each error includes field name and constraint
disallowed_assumptions: 400 returned instead of 422 for validation failures
single generic validation error message returned
validation errors returned one at a time not all at once
review_notes: Both the HTTP status (422 not 400) and the structured error format are explicit requirements. Multiple simultaneous validation errors must all appear in the response.

gold_test_cases.title: Missing required field returns HTTP 422
preconditions: API is running
An endpoint with required fields exists
steps: Send a POST request with a required field omitted
gold_test_cases.expected_result: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity is returned

gold_test_cases.title: Validation error response lists all failing fields
preconditions: API is running
steps: Send a request with multiple validation failures (missing field and wrong type)
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Response body contains a structured array listing every validation error including field name, invalid value, and constraint violated

gold_test_cases.title: Wrong field type returns 422 with field-level error
preconditions: API is running
steps: Send a request with a string value where an integer is expected
gold_test_cases.expected_result: HTTP 422 is returned with an error entry that names the field and describes the type constraint

disallowed_assumptions: export omits columns without warning
export fails silently for large datasets
no performance requirement for export
review_notes: Two categories: functional (columns, header) and performance (10k tasks / 30 sec). Must cover both. The column list is exhaustive in the requirement.

gold_test_cases.title: CSV export includes correct header row
preconditions: A project exists with at least one task
steps: Click export button in project view
gold_test_cases.expected_result: First row contains column headers: task ID, title, status, assignee, due date, created date

gold_test_cases.title: Each task appears as one row with all required columns populated
preconditions: A project exists with tasks that have assignees and due dates
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Each task is present as one row with all six columns populated

gold_test_cases.title: Export of 10000 tasks completes within 30 seconds
preconditions: A project with 10000 tasks exists
steps: Measure time until file download completes
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Export completes and file is available within 30 seconds

disallowed_assumptions: JSON schema differs from API tasks list schema
empty project returns null instead of empty array
filename format not enforced
review_notes: Schema compliance with the API list endpoint is the key constraint. Empty project behavior is an explicit edge case.

gold_test_cases.title: JSON export produces a file conforming to API tasks list schema
preconditions: A project with tasks exists
steps: Validate the file against the documented API tasks list schema
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Exported JSON is valid and matches the API tasks list endpoint schema

gold_test_cases.title: Exporting an empty project produces an empty JSON array
preconditions: A project with no tasks exists
steps: Export the empty project as JSON
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Downloaded file contains an empty JSON array []

gold_test_cases.title: Exported JSON filename follows the required pattern
preconditions: A project named my-project exists
steps: Export the project tasks as JSON
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Filename matches the pattern my-project-tasks-YYYY-MM-DD.json

required_coverage_points: only one concurrent export per user

only one concurrent export per user: one active export at a time

disallowed_assumptions: export processed synchronously for all dataset sizes
download link never expires
multiple concurrent exports per user are unlimited
review_notes: Multi-condition async flow. Six distinct requirements. The concurrent export limit and 48-hour expiry are explicit constraints that may be overlooked.

gold_test_cases.title: Export of large dataset returns immediate acknowledgment with job ID
preconditions: A dataset with more than 50000 rows is available for export
steps: Trigger export of the large dataset
gold_test_cases.expected_result: API returns an immediate 202 Accepted response with a job ID; export has not yet completed

gold_test_cases.title: User receives email and in-app notification when export is ready
preconditions: A large export job has completed
steps: Wait for the export job to finish
gold_test_cases.expected_result: User receives both an email notification and an in-app notification that the export is ready

gold_test_cases.title: Export download link expires after 48 hours
preconditions: An export has been generated
steps: Wait more than 48 hours after export generation
Attempt to use the download link
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Download link returns a 404 or expiry error after 48 hours

gold_test_cases.title: Second concurrent export request is rejected
preconditions: User already has one active export job in progress
steps: Attempt to trigger a second export
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Second export request is rejected with an error indicating one export is already active

required_coverage_points: non-ASCII project name in export filename
disallowed_assumptions: non-ASCII characters stripped from filename silently
Content-Disposition header uses unencoded raw UTF-8 bytes
only ASCII project names are supported
review_notes: Adversarial input test with specific technical requirement (RFC 5987). Must test multiple non-ASCII scripts. Flag if model does not mention Content-Disposition encoding.

gold_test_cases.title: Export from project with Chinese name produces correctly encoded filename
preconditions: A project exists with a Chinese character name such as 项目一
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Header uses RFC 5987 encoding (filename* parameter) and the filename contains the correct encoded characters

gold_test_cases.title: Export filename with emoji is handled correctly by browser
preconditions: A project exists with an emoji in its name
steps: Export project data in a modern browser
gold_test_cases.expected_result: File is saved with a valid filename; no browser error occurs

gold_test_cases.title: Raw unencoded UTF-8 is not used in Content-Disposition header
preconditions: A project with non-ASCII name exists
steps: Inspect the raw Content-Disposition response header
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Header uses RFC 5987 encoding (filename*=UTF-8'' prefix); raw UTF-8 bytes are not used directly

required_coverage_points: email failure does not block registration
disallowed_assumptions: registration fails if welcome email cannot be sent
welcome email sent after more than 24 hours
email content not personalized
review_notes: The fault-tolerance requirement (email failure must not block registration) is the key non-obvious test case. Most models will miss it.

gold_test_cases.title: Welcome email is sent after successful registration
preconditions: Email service is available
steps: Complete new user registration with a valid email and display name
Check the registered email inbox within 5 minutes
gold_test_cases.expected_result: A welcome email arrives within 5 minutes containing the user's display name and a getting started guide link

gold_test_cases.title: Registration succeeds even if welcome email fails to send
preconditions: Email service is unavailable or returns an error
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Registration is successful and the user can log in; the email failure is logged but does not block account creation

gold_test_cases.title: Welcome email contains display name and required links
preconditions: A new user has just registered
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Email contains the user's display name, a link to the getting started guide, and a link to profile settings

required_coverage_points: covers three steps project member task
resumes from last step within 30 days
disallowed_assumptions: wizard shown on every login
progress lost on browser close
wizard required before any system access
review_notes: Multi-step flow with persistence. Must cover the resume-after-return scenario explicitly. Wizard must not be shown on second and subsequent logins if completed.

gold_test_cases.title: Onboarding wizard is shown on first login only
preconditions: New user account created
User has never logged in before
steps: Log in for the first time
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Onboarding wizard is displayed after first login

gold_test_cases.title: Wizard progress is saved and can be resumed within 30 days
preconditions: New user has completed the first wizard step
steps: Close the browser without completing the wizard
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Wizard resumes from the second step; first step is shown as completed

gold_test_cases.title: Completing all wizard steps shows a confirmation screen
preconditions: User is on the final wizard step
gold_test_cases.expected_result: A confirmation screen is displayed indicating the wizard is complete

disallowed_assumptions: skipping wizard restricts feature access
skipped wizard reappears on next login
skip requires special permission or admin approval
review_notes: Severely under-specified on what 'skip' means for individual steps vs the full wizard. Good output will note the ambiguity and test both cases. Key anti-patterns: access restriction after skip and re-display on next login.

gold_test_cases.title: User can skip an individual wizard step
preconditions: User is on the onboarding wizard
A skip button is available on the current step
steps: Click skip on an individual step
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The step is marked as skipped and the wizard advances to the next step

gold_test_cases.title: User can skip the entire wizard
preconditions: User is on any step of the onboarding wizard
steps: Click the option to skip the entire wizard
gold_test_cases.expected_result: The entire wizard is dismissed and the user is taken to the main application

gold_test_cases.title: Full system access is available immediately after skipping the wizard
preconditions: User has just skipped the entire wizard
steps: Attempt to access any system feature
gold_test_cases.expected_result: All system features are fully accessible with no restrictions

gold_test_cases.title: Skipped wizard does not reappear on subsequent logins
preconditions: User previously skipped the onboarding wizard
steps: Log out and log back in
gold_test_cases.expected_result: Onboarding wizard is not displayed on the subsequent login
